skill.name: close-automation
skill.description: Automate Close CRM tasks through Rube MCP, including lead creation, call logging, SMS handling, tasks, notes, and selected activity deletion.
skill.summary: Automates common Close CRM workflows through the Rube MCP Close toolkit.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, CRM identifiers, and documentation text. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill depends on a third-party MCP endpoint and can trigger Close CRM actions that send messages or delete call records. Publish with a clear warning about external service trust, CRM data exposure, and destructive operations.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Handles CRM Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and authenticate Close through that integration. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it means CRM data and actions depend on a third-party network service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and Close connection requirement are explicit in the setup instructions. This is not malicious by itself, but it creates a real trust and data-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: CRM Actions Can Send Messages or Delete Records
medium_findings.description: The documented workflows include sending SMS messages and deleting Close call activities. These are intended CRM functions, but misuse can create external communications or permanent loss of activity history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SMS and delete-call workflows are plainly documented, including a note that deletion is permanent. The risk is operational rather than hidden or malicious.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings. Reviewed lines use Markdown backticks for Rube and Close tool names, parameter names, and fenced explanatory examples; no executable script or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation containing tool slugs and parameter names. No Ruby code, shell syntax, script file, or command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to the skill description and a task text parameter. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing functions, encryption settings, or security-sensitive algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language CRM documentation and parameter descriptions. They do not contain crypto APIs or algorithm configuration.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are CRM Identifier References
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged identifier-like text such as lead_id, contact_id, note_id, and call_id. These are Close CRM object identifiers used for normal workflow documentation, not host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text discusses Close lead, contact, note, and call IDs. No commands collect system information, enumerate files, inspect environment variables, or probe networks.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Service Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires users to configure an external MCP endpoint for Close CRM automation. This is a valid integration pattern but should be treated as a network trust boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded endpoint is explicitly documented. The security concern is data exposure to the configured MCP provider, not hidden code execution.

content.value_statement: Sales teams lose time when routine Close CRM updates require repeated manual steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for leads, calls, SMS, tasks, notes, and call deletion.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for a Rube MCP Close connection.
Creates Close leads with contacts, custom fields, and status IDs.
Logs inbound and outbound call activities with duration and notes.
Creates SMS activities with lead, contact, direction, text, and status fields.
Creates follow-up tasks tied to Close lead records.
Retrieves notes and deletes call activities when valid IDs are provided.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP connection and authenticated Close account.
Depends on current tool schemas returned by RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS.
Does not perform automatic duplicate lead detection.
Deletion and outbound messaging should be confirmed before execution.

use_cases.title: Create Qualified Leads
use_cases.description: Add new company leads with contact details, status IDs, and required custom fields.

use_cases.title: Log Sales Activity
use_cases.description: Record calls, SMS messages, and notes against the correct Close lead and contact.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Follow-Ups
use_cases.description: Create tasks with due dates and assignees after calls or message exchanges.

prompt_templates.title: Check Close Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the close-automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and that my Close connection is ACTIVE.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Close lead for this company and contact. Search the current Close tool schema first, then ask me for any missing required fields.

prompt_templates.title: Log a Call and Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Log this completed outbound call in Close, include the notes and duration, then create a follow-up task for the assigned owner.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Execute Activity Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Close call activity I describe, show me the call ID and impact, then wait for confirmation before deleting it.

output_examples.input: Create a lead for Acme Robotics with Jane Lee as the primary contact.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the current Close schema, asks for missing required fields, then creates the lead with the contact nested under the company record.

output_examples.input: Log a 12 minute outbound call and create a follow-up for Friday.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the lead ID, records the call duration in seconds, adds notes, and creates a dated task for the selected assignee.

output_examples.input: Delete the duplicate call record from yesterday.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the matching call record, explains that deletion is permanent, and waits for explicit confirmation before using the delete action.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before each workflow so schemas match the current integration.
Confirm lead, contact, and activity IDs before creating or deleting records.
Ask for explicit approval before outbound SMS or permanent deletion.
anti_patterns: Do not create duplicate leads without searching Close first.
Do not use contact names when Close requires contact IDs.
Do not delete call records based only on vague descriptions.

faq.question: Does this skill require Close authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The Rube MCP Close connection must be active before workflows can run.

faq.question: Can it create contacts directly?
faq.answer: It documents contacts as nested objects under Close leads, so lead context is required.

faq.question: Can it send SMS messages?
faq.answer: Yes, if the Close account has SMS capability configured and the user approves the message.

faq.question: Can it prevent duplicate leads?
faq.answer: No. It warns that duplicate detection is not automatic, so users should search before creating leads.

faq.question: Can deleted calls be restored?
faq.answer: The skill states that call deletion is permanent and cannot be undone.

faq.question: Why should schemas be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change, so searching first helps use the correct parameters.
